2605.18111.title: How Good LLMs Are at Answering Bangla Medical Visual Questions? Dataset and Benchmarking
2605.18111.paper_summary: Recent advancements in Large Language Models (LLMs) and Large Vision Language Models (LVLMs) have enabled general-purpose systems to demonstrate promising capabilities in complex reasoning tasks, including those in the medical domain. Medical Visual Question Answering (MedVQA) has particularly benefited from these developments. However, despite Bangla being one of the most widely spoken languages globally, there exists no established MedVQA benchmark for it. To address this gap, we introduce BanglaMedVQA, a dataset comprising clinically validated image-question-answer pairs, along with a comprehensive evaluation of current foundation models on this resource. Consistent with prior findings that report low performance of current models on English MedVQA benchmarks, our analysis reveals that Bangla performance is substantially lower, reflecting the challenges inherent to low-resource languages. Even top-performing models such as Gemini and GPT-4.1 mini fail to accurately answer specialized diagnostic questions, indicating severe limitations in fine-grained medical reasoning. Although certain open-source models, such as Gemma-3, occasionally outperform these models in general categories, they too struggle with clinically complex questions, underscoring the urgent need for top-notch evaluation method.
2605.18111.paper_summary_zh: 最近在大型語言模型（LLMs）和大型視覺語言模型（LVLMs）方面的進展，使得通用系統在複雜推理任務中展現出有希望的能力，包括醫療領域的任務。醫療視覺問題回答（MedVQA）特別受益於這些發展。然而，儘管孟加拉語是全球使用最廣泛的語言之一，但目前尚未建立針對該語言的MedVQA基準。為了解決這一空白，我們推出了BanglaMedVQA，這是一個包含臨床驗證的圖像-問題-答案對的數據集，以及對當前基礎模型在該資源上的全面評估。與先前報告當前模型在英語MedVQA基準上表現不佳的發現一致，我們的分析顯示，孟加拉語的表現顯著更低，反映出低資源語言固有的挑戰。即使是表現最佳的模型，如Gemini和GPT-4.1 mini，也無法準確回答專業的診斷問題，顯示出在細緻醫療推理方面的嚴重限制。儘管某些開源模型，如Gemma-3，偶爾在一般類別中表現超過這些模型，但它們在臨床複雜問題上也面臨挑戰，強調了對高品質評估方法的迫切需求。
2605.18111.author: Rafid Ahmed et.al.
2605.18111.authors: Rafid Ahmed, Intesar Tahmid, Mir Sazzat Hossain, Tasnimul Hossain Tomal, Md Fahim, Md Farhad Alam Bhuiyan
